title: Famous Places of India
description: A stronger India landmarks round with monuments, caves, temples, forts, and UNESCO-rich locations.

questions.prompt: Which Mughal monument near Agra was built by Emperor Akbar as a gateway to his new capital city?
questions.hint: It is one of the highest gateways in the world.

questions.prompt: The group of rock-cut monuments at Mahabalipuram lies in which present-day state?
questions.hint: Its capital is Chennai.

questions.prompt: Which temple complex in Karnataka is known for the stone chariot that appears on the 50-rupee note?
questions.hint: It was once the ceremonial centre of the Vijayanagara Empire.

questions.prompt: Which fort in Rajasthan is nicknamed Sonar Quila because of its golden-yellow sandstone?
questions.hint: It rises directly from the Thar Desert.

questions.prompt: Which cave site in Maharashtra is famous for ancient Buddhist murals and paintings?
questions.hint: It is paired in quiz questions with Ellora but is older in style.

questions.prompt: Which city is home to the Charminar?
questions.hint: It is now the capital of Telangana.

questions.prompt: Which Odisha temple is designed in the shape of a colossal chariot of the Sun God?
questions.hint: It is a UNESCO World Heritage Site on the eastern coast.

questions.prompt: Which fort near Aurangabad is famous for a moat, seven layers of defense, and a cannon called Mendha Tope?
questions.hint: It shares its name with a film about bravery from the Deccan.

questions.prompt: Which monument in Delhi was built by Qutb al-Din Aibak and later completed by Iltutmish?
questions.hint: It is a towering minaret complex.

questions.prompt: Which hill station in West Bengal is famous for the toy train and views of Kanchenjunga?
questions.hint: It is also associated with tea estates.

questions.prompt: Which island monument in Mumbai was formerly called Victoria Terminus and is now a UNESCO-listed railway station?
questions.hint: Its new name honors a Maratha ruler.

questions.prompt: Which cave complex near Mumbai is famous for a massive three-faced sculpture of Shiva?
questions.hint: It is on an island once called Gharapuri.

questions.prompt: Which temple town on the banks of the Hooghly is famous for the Dakshineswar Kali Temple?
questions.hint: Sri Ramakrishna served there.

questions.prompt: Which city in Rajasthan is known as the City of Lakes?
questions.hint: Its palaces overlook Lake Pichola.

questions.prompt: Which ghat town in Uttarakhand is the starting point for the trek to Valley of Flowers?
questions.hint: It is also linked to Hemkund Sahib.

questions.prompt: Which structure in Sanchi is among the oldest stone monuments in India and was commissioned originally by Ashoka?
questions.hint: It is a Buddhist hemispherical structure.

questions.prompt: Which fort in Maharashtra served as the capital of Chhatrapati Shivaji Maharaj's kingdom for a period?
questions.hint: It is the site of his coronation.

questions.prompt: Which Punjabi shrine is also called Harmandir Sahib?
questions.hint: It is the holiest gurdwara of Sikhism.

questions.prompt: Which stepwell in Gujarat is a UNESCO site noted for intricate sculptural panels and inverted-temple design?
questions.hint: It is in Patan.

questions.prompt: Which palace in Mysuru is illuminated during Dasara and replaced an older wooden palace?
questions.hint: It is also called Amba Vilas Palace.

questions.prompt: Which monastery in Ladakh sits dramatically on a hilltop and is often compared visually to a mini-fortress?
questions.hint: Its name begins with T and it is near Leh.

questions.prompt: Which temple at Madurai is dedicated chiefly to Goddess Meenakshi and Lord Sundareshwarar?
questions.hint: It is famous for towering gopurams.

questions.prompt: Which fort in Delhi was built by Shah Jahan as the seat of Mughal power?
questions.hint: The Prime Minister addresses the nation there on Independence Day.

questions.prompt: Which cave shrine in Jammu and Kashmir is one of India's most-visited Hindu pilgrimage destinations?
questions.hint: Pilgrims trek to a naturally formed ice lingam.

questions.prompt: Which Kerala backwater destination is famous for houseboats and the Nehru Trophy Boat Race?
questions.hint: Its name begins with A.

questions.prompt: Which temple in Thanjavur was built by Raja Raja Chola I and is also called Peruvudaiyar Kovil?
questions.hint: It is a major Chola UNESCO monument.

questions.prompt: Which fort in Bihar, associated with Sher Shah Suri, sits atop a hill near Sasaram and commands a dramatic reservoir?
questions.hint: It shares its name with a popular Hindi phrase for a lion-like fort.

questions.prompt: Which Portuguese-era basilica in Goa houses the relics of St. Francis Xavier?
questions.hint: It is a UNESCO-listed church in Old Goa.

questions.prompt: Which fort-palace complex in Rajasthan is known for the Sheesh Mahal and overlooks Maota Lake?
questions.hint: It lies near Jaipur.

questions.prompt: Which Buddhist university ruins in Bihar are associated with ancient scholars like Xuanzang's accounts?
questions.hint: They are near Rajgir.

questions.prompt: Which temple town in Odisha is famous for the Jagannath Temple and the annual Rath Yatra?
questions.hint: It is one corner of the Char Dham circuit.

questions.prompt: Which marble memorial in Kolkata was built in honor of Queen Victoria?
questions.hint: It now functions as a museum.

questions.prompt: Which site in Madhya Pradesh preserves prehistoric rock shelters with ancient paintings?
questions.hint: It is near Bhopal and starts with B.

questions.prompt: Which minaret-city complex in Bijapur is famous for its whispering gallery beneath a vast dome?
questions.hint: It is a mausoleum of Mohammed Adil Shah.

questions.prompt: Which island in the Bay of Bengal is known for the Cellular Jail?
questions.hint: It is the administrative centre of the Andaman and Nicobar Islands.

questions.prompt: Which riverfront ghat city is famous for the Kashi Vishwanath Temple?
questions.hint: It is one of the oldest continuously inhabited cities in the world.

questions.prompt: Which palace-fort in Gwalior is linked with Man Singh Tomar and blue tile decoration?
questions.hint: It dominates the city from a sandstone hill.

questions.prompt: Which UNESCO site in Assam protects the great one-horned rhinoceros?
questions.hint: It is a national park on the Brahmaputra floodplains.

questions.prompt: Which shrine in Ajmer is the dargah of Khwaja Moinuddin Chishti?
questions.hint: It is a major Sufi pilgrimage centre.

questions.prompt: Which beach town in Karnataka is famous for the Mahabaleshwar Temple and a Shiva linga said to be Atmalinga?
questions.hint: It lies on the Karavali coast.

questions.prompt: Which temple complex in Khajuraho is best known for ornate Nagara-style sculpture?
questions.hint: It is in Madhya Pradesh.

questions.prompt: Which fort on an island off the Murud coast in Maharashtra was considered nearly unconquerable?
questions.hint: It was built by the Siddis.

questions.prompt: Which palace in Jaipur is famous for its facade of many small windows designed for royal women to observe the street?
questions.hint: Its name translates to Palace of Winds.

questions.prompt: Which Tamil Nadu shore temple site was built by the Pallavas and faces the Bay of Bengal?
questions.hint: It is in Mamallapuram.

questions.prompt: Which monastery in Sikkim is among the most important seats of the Karma Kagyu school?
questions.hint: It is in Rumtek, near Gangtok.

questions.prompt: Which cenotaph complex in Jodhpur is built from intricately carved sandstone and overlooks Mehrangarh?
questions.hint: It was built in memory of Maharaja Jaswant Singh II.

questions.prompt: Which lake palace in Udaipur now operates as a luxury hotel on Lake Pichola?
questions.hint: It was built by Maharana Jagat Singh II.

questions.prompt: Which monument in Bihar marks the spot where the Buddha is believed to have attained enlightenment?
questions.hint: It is in Bodh Gaya.

questions.prompt: Which Andhra Pradesh hill shrine dedicated to Venkateswara is among the wealthiest temples in the world?
questions.hint: It is in Tirupati district.

questions.prompt: Which fort in Chittorgarh is associated with Padmini, Rana Kumbha, and repeated sieges?
questions.hint: It is one of the largest forts in India.
